title: airDrop function allows NFT theft via malicious xAirDrop contract
reason: Unsupported: `airDrop()` is atomic, so if `xAirDrop` fails to return the NFT the final transfer reverts and the initial transfer to `xAirDrop` is rolled back.

title: setXAirDrop allows admin to set malicious contract to steal all NFTs
reason: Unsupported as written: a malicious `xAirDrop` alone cannot keep NFTs because the full `airDrop()` transaction reverts unless the NFT is returned; remaining admin-risk is already captured by the broader arbitrary-call finding.

title: Missing nonReentrant modifier in notifyOrderLiquidated allows reentrancy attack
reason: Not substantiated: the function is controller-only, updates liquidation state before the transfer, and no realistic callback path was found that lets the recipient steal assets or corrupt accounting.

title: Missing nonReentrant modifier in notifyRepayBorrow allows reentrancy attack
reason: Not substantiated: although the transfer happens before `isWithdraw` is set, ownership has already moved during the ERC721 callback, so a reentrant second withdrawal/transfer would fail rather than extract extra assets.

title: setPunks allows admin to set malicious punk contracts to steal user NFTs
reason: Primarily a privileged configuration/trust issue and weaker than the accepted admin arbitrary-call/withdraw findings, which already let the admin extract escrowed assets directly.

title: No minimum price protection in auction allows unfair liquidation
reason: This describes protocol auction design rather than a concrete implementation bug; no incorrect accounting or broken invariant was identified.

title: No slippage protection in doTransferIn for ERC20 tokens
reason: Incorrect: `doTransferIn()` explicitly checks that the balance delta equals `amount`, so fee-on-transfer/deflationary tokens revert instead of causing silent accounting drift.

title: Division before multiplication can cause precision loss in auctionAllowed
reason: Incorrect: the code computes `lastPrice.mul(_increasingMin).div(1e18)`, so multiplication already occurs before division.

title: Fixed gas limit in ETH transfer may cause transfer failures
reason: As framed, this only affects the caller-controlled ETH change refund path in `doTransferIn()`; the reportable protocol-level DoS is the separate accepted finding about `doTransferOut()` using `transfer` for settlement payouts.

title: No validation that collection whitelist settings are reasonable
reason: This is an admin-parameterization concern, not a standalone implementation vulnerability.
